item.title: A World Trade Organization is irrelevant when no one follows its rules
item.description: The most recent stalemate between the United States and Brazil at the World Trade Organization’s conference reveals how vulnerable the trade body has become.

item.title: The joy of Artemis II shows why going back to the moon matters
item.description: Artemis II, air travel and national debt, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: Will the next William Henry Harrison please stand up?
item.description: A candidate in the mold of our ninth and short-serving president is the perfect response to Trump.

item.title: Maine’s race to the top on taxes
item.description: After opposing similar tax hikes in her state, Janet Mills is supporting a millionaires tax for Maine as she runs against Graham Platner in the Senate primary.

item.title: Can Trump end the war in Iran and save face? I answered your questions.
item.description: David’s live chat with readers started at 12 p.m. ET on Monday. Read the transcript.

item.title: Congress is threatening a leading source of single-family homes
item.description: A great, bipartisan housing bill could undermine its own objective.

item.title: What Elon Musk can do for Johnny Q. Public
item.description: Cut ordinary Americans in on the SpaceX IPO and watch opinions of capitalism skyrocket.

item.title: Hungary is a laboratory for illiberal nationalism. The results are in.
item.description: A 16-year experiment has yielded none of Viktor Orban’s stated goals.

item.title: As a Marine veteran, I see America’s honor in Markwayne Mullin’s hands
item.description: The Afghans who fought alongside the U.S. deserve dignity, not an immigration crackdown.

item.title: As the Iran war continues, these ‘swing states’ will be pivotal
item.description: The conflict in Iran has boosted the strategic value of overland energy routes across Eurasia.

item.title: Schools are closing for the NFL draft. What does this teach students?
item.description: Pittsburgh prides itself on showing up and working hard. Students should, too.

item.title: Virginia’s new maps show a partisan power grab. Will voters mind?
item.description: Readers on airlines and pilots, and Virginia redistricting in the eyes of voters,

item.title: How Congress could put the Constitution in every American’s pocket
item.description: The Liberty Dollar Bill Act would be a step toward national unity.

item.title: Taiwan’s self-defense paradox
item.description: While U.S. senators lobbied for a Taiwan defense spending increase, the opposition leader said she’s heading to China. Beijing will exploit such clear disunity.

item.title: I told the internet I use AI. Boy, was it mad.
item.description: Artificial intelligence helps you work harder, instead of just outsourcing your brain.

item.title: The hazard of D.C.’s utility shutoff moratorium
item.description: Janeese Lewis George’s idea to address rising energy bills creates a moral hazard.

item.title: Veterans spending has surged. Are veterans better off?
item.description: Two decades of increases in Veterans Affairs spending has little to show for it besides a much larger bureaucracy.
